channel.title: Bloomberg Technology
channel.copyright: Copyright 2025 BLOOMBERG L.P. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

item.title: Tencent’s Revenue Beats Estimates in Boost for AI Ambitions
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd.’s revenue climbed a better-than-anticipated 15%, bolstering investor expectations that its expanding gaming and social media portfolio will propel its efforts in a global AI race.
item.dc:creator: Zheping Huang

media:content.media:description: The market is looking forward to the launch of Valorant Mobile, a highly-anticipated smartphone version of Riot Games Inc.’s seminal title. Source: Riot Games

item.title: Right-Wing Creators Seize on UK's Anti-Immigrant Protests
item.description: Misleading information spread online by right-wing “influencers" attending UK anti-immigrant protests this summer is adding to pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer, as he struggles to reduce illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Zuidijk, Lucy White

media:content.media:description: Police officers scuffle with protesters outside the Bell Hotel in Epping, England on July 20.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Iluvatar CoreX Is Said to Mull Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Iluvatar CoreX is considering an initial public offering in Hong Kong, as investor interest in Chinese artificial intelligence chipmakers builds with Beijing discouraging local firms from using Nvidia Corp. processors.
item.dc:creator: Julia Fioretti, Zheping Huang, Dong Cao

media:content.media:description: Iluvatar CoreX products.

item.title: CoreWeave Rally Sputters as AI Buildout Costs Widen Losses
item.description: CoreWeave Inc.’s shares fell 10% in after-market trading following a disappointing earnings outlook, reflecting margin pressures from a rapid AI datacenter expansion.
item.dc:creator: Redd Brown, Vlad Savov

media:content.media:description: CoreWeave Inc. signage in Times Square in New York, US, on Friday, May 9, 2025. After a heavily scaled down initial public offering, CoreWeave Inc. is in discussions to raise about $1.5 billion in debt, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: German Military Targets 8,300 Drone Systems, Lagging NATO Allies
item.description: The German military is looking to secure 8,300 drone systems by the end of the decade, far fewer than some of its NATO allies aim for, as spending on unmanned defense systems and autonomous weapons soars globally.
item.dc:creator: Christina Kyriasoglou, Michael Nienaber

media:content.media:description: German plans call for an arsenal of about 5,700 unmanned aerial systems, 560 interceptors and 2,070 loitering munition systems in 2029.

item.title: Temasek-Backed ST Telemedia Is Said to Consider Selling Stake in Data Center Operator GDS
item.description: ST Telemedia is considering selling its holding in Chinese data center operator GDS Holdings Ltd., people familiar with the situation said.
item.dc:creator: Dong Cao, Elffie Chew

media:content.media:description: Data center.

item.title: Evolution Games Ran in Banned Markets, Execs Say in Secret Tapes
item.description: Current and former executives at Stockholm-based online gambling company Evolution AB were secretly filmed describing how its casino games made their way illegally to countries such as Iran, Sudan and China, according to a filing submitted to a New Jersey court on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Solon

media:content.media:description: A ball sits on a roulette wheel.

item.title: South Africa, ArcelorMittal Locked in Talks Over Mills’ Fate
item.description: South Africa’s government is locked in crisis talks with the local unit of ArcelorMittal SA to decide the fate of the company’s loss-making construction-steel mills, people familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Loni Prinsloo, Antony Sguazzin

media:content.media:description: An ArcelorMittal steel mill in Vanderbijlpark, South Africa

item.title: Musk Loses Court Bid to Dismiss OpenAI’s Harassment Claim
item.description: Elon Musk must face claims by OpenAI that his attacks on the startup in court and in the media amount to a “years-long harassment campaign,” a federal judge ruled.
item.dc:creator: Robert Burnson

media:content.media:description: Elon Musk

item.title: Tencent Investors Eye Path to Record in Cheap Stock Valuations
item.description: As tech megacaps around the world climb to new records, investors see a chance for Tencent Holdings Ltd. shares to finally regain their former glory.
item.dc:creator: Jeanny Yu

media:content.media:description: Signage for Tencent Holdings Ltd. at the company's headquarters building in Shenzhen, China, on Wednesday, April 17, 2024. Tencent plans to more than double its stock buyback program to at least $12.8 billion in 2024, mollifying investors concerned about a gradual dissipation of growth during a Chinese economic downturn.

item.title: SoftBank Plans ¥100 Billion Subordinated Bond Deal Amid AI Drive
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to sell about ¥100 billion ($676 million) of subordinated bonds, taking advantage of improved sentiment on plans to kick-start its stalled AI drive.
item.dc:creator: Takahiko Hyuga

media:content.media:description: SoftBank branding.

item.title: Taiwan’s Stock Index Rises to Near Record High Amid AI Optimism
item.description: Taiwanese shares neared a record high, buoyed by a strong outlook for artificial intelligence and exemption from some US tariffs for major chip makers.
item.dc:creator: Jeanny Yu

media:content.media:description: An image of a semiconductor wafer displayed at the TSMC Museum of Innovation in Hsinchu, Taiwan. Photographer: An Rong Xu/Bloomberg

item.title: Carlyle Nears $1 Billion Sale of Tech Services Firm HSO to Bain
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. is close to selling HSO to Bain Capital in a deal valuing the Dutch technology services company at about $1 billion, according to people with knowledge of the matter.
item.dc:creator: Swetha Gopinath

item.title: Pony AI, Nearing Full-Year Robotaxi Goal, Eyes European Markets
item.description: Pony AI Inc. is confident it will meet its 2025 robotaxi vehicle output target and is actively exploring new markets in Europe, even as the industry’s commercialization develops at an uneven pace across different regions.
item.dc:creator: Linda Lew, Rachel Yeo

media:content.media:description: A Pony.ai Inc. autonomous vehicle in Shanghai. Photographer: Qilai Shen/Bloomberg

item.title: Paytm Shares Surge After RBI Grants Payment Aggregator License
item.description: Shares of One 97 Communications Ltd. surged to the highest level in over three years after its unit received approval from India’s central bank to operate as an online payment aggregator.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gabriel Simon

media:content.media:description: A QR code for the Paytm digital payment system at a roadside vegetables stall in Mumbai, India.

item.title: Mexico’s Stori Eyes IPO by 2027 With Trend Toward Profits
item.description: Mexican fintech Stori is betting that it can be ready for an initial public offering in 24 months after reaching sustainable profitability this year, Chief Executive Officer Bin Chen said.
item.dc:creator: Maria Clara Cobo, Kelsey Butler

media:content.media:description: Stori CEO Bin Chen Source: Stori

item.title: Sea Sales Top Estimates as Online Shoppers Keep Spending
item.description: Sea Ltd.’s soaring sales has brought it close to reclaiming its title as Southeast Asia’s most valuable company, with the stock’s 19% jump in New York on Tuesday leaving its market capitalization near that of DBS Group Holdings Ltd.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Poh, Benicia Tan

media:content.media:description: A sorting facility of Sea's SPX in Singapore.

item.title: ULA’s Vulcan Rocket Launches First National Security Mission
item.description: United Launch Alliance launched its first national security mission with the newly developed Vulcan rocket, a key test of the vehicle’s ability to put satellites into high orbits above the Earth.
item.dc:creator: Loren Grush

media:content.media:description: The ULA Vulcan rocket in Cape Canaveral, Florida, US, on Aug. 11. Photographer: Scott Schilke/Sipa/AP Photo

item.title: Circle Falls After Company, Holders Offer 10 Million Shares
item.description: Shares of Circle Internet Group Inc. fell after the second-largest stablecoin issuer and a group of shareholders including co-founder and Chief Executive Officer Jeremy Allaire announced an offering to sell a combined 10 million shares.
item.dc:creator: Monique Mulima, Anthony Hughes

media:content.media:description: Traders work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) during the Circle Internet Financial Ltd. initial public offering (IPO) in New York, US, on Thursday, June 5, 2025. Circle shares surged as much as 235% after the company and some of its shareholders raised nearly $1.1 billion in an initial public offering that was upsized twice amid strong demand. Photographer: Michael Nagle/Bloomberg

item.title: AI Eroded Doctors’ Ability to Spot Cancer Within Months in Study
item.description: Artificial intelligence, touted for its potential to transform medicine, led to some doctors losing skills after just a few months in a new study.
item.dc:creator: Harry Black

media:content.media:description: An examination room is seen at the Oscar Center in the Brooklyn borough of New York, U.S., on Wednesday, Dec. 7, 2016. The Oscar Center runs in partnership with Mount Sinai Health Systems providing primary care services and health and wellness programs. Photographer: Kholood Eid/Bloomberg

item.title: Musk Accuses Apple of Unfairly Favoring OpenAI on iPhone
item.description: Elon Musk lashed out against Apple Inc.’s app store practices late on Monday, accusing the iPhone maker of favoring OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Vlad Savov

item.title: Falabella’s Second Quarter Profit Rises on Retail Unit Strength
item.description: Falabella SA’s second-quarter profit rose more than expected as retail sales across the economy in Chile remain robust, even as other indicators show more subdued expansion.
item.dc:creator: Carolina Gonzalez

media:content.media:description: A Falabella store in Santiago, Chile, on Thursday, Sept. 7, 2023. What was once one of the biggest retail groups in Chile, Falabella faces a potential credit downgrade after spending big on a push into e-commerce.

item.title: China Urges Local Firms to Avoid Nvidia H20 Chips | Bloomberg Tech 8/12/2025
item.description: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss reports that China is urging its domestic companies to avoid using Nvidia H20 chips. Plus, Elon Musk accuses Apple of favoring OpenAI above other AI companies in its app store, even threatening possible legal action. And Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire discusses plans for new products as shares of the company soar following its earnings report. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Opendoor Rallies as Crypto Influencer Pompliano Buys Shares
item.description: Opendoor Technologies Inc. has resumed its surge after cryptocurrency investor and influential newsletter writer and podcaster Anthony Pompliano said he’d bought shares in the digital real estate firm.
item.dc:creator: Norah Mulinda

item.title: Albemarle Lithium Plant Is Operating After Incident in Chile
item.description: Albemarle Corp. experienced an incident at its lithium-processing plant in Chile last week, which is the subject of an investigation, the latest threat to global supply of the battery metal.
item.dc:creator: James Attwood

item.title: DuPont Spinoff Qnity Prices $4.1 Billion in Debt for Payout
item.description: DuPont de Nemours Inc.’s electronics business, which will be spun off into the Qnity Electronics Inc. brand, priced a $4.1 billion debt offering to fund a payout to DuPont in connection with the reorganization.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Graf

media:content.media:description: Dupont corporate headquarters in Wilmington, Delaware. Photographer: Mark Makela/Getty Images

item.title: AI Startup Perplexity Makes $34.5 Billion Bid for Google’s Chrome Browser
item.description: AI startup Perplexity made a formal offer to acquire Google’s Chrome browser for $34.5 billion, an audacious bid to get ahead of a potential requirement for the search giant to sell the web browser in US antitrust proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Julia Love

media:content.media:description: The Google Chrome web browser app. Photographer: Chesnot/Getty Images

item.title: CoreWeave Traders Emboldened by AI Spending Ahead of Earnings
item.description: The race to build computing capacity for artificial intelligence is reviving bullish bets on one of Wall Street’s most contentious stocks: CoreWeave Inc.
item.dc:creator: Carmen Reinicke

item.title: India’s First AI Unicorn Fractal Files for $560 Million IPO
item.description: Fractal Analytics, a provider of artificial intelligence and analytics services, filed for an initial public offering in Mumbai that could value the company at more than $3.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Saritha Rai

media:content.media:description: The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) building in Mumbai, India, on Friday, March 15, 2024. A sharp correction in small-cap stocks portends souring risk appetite on the broader Indian market, which is now an underperformer in Asia Pacific after a multi-year rally. Photographer: Dhiraj Singh/Bloomberg

item.title: Billions Wiped Out as Software Sinks on AI Disruption Fear
item.description: Growing worries that artificial intelligence tools could soon disrupt the world’s biggest software businesses are sparking a selloff across the sector.
item.dc:creator: Henry Ren
